metadata.notes: agent=yes (vibe renders a TUI-only tool-permission dialog); daemon=bug (read-back path exists but is gated on the control consent vibe never declares + DetectUI lacks vibe's markers — both feasible fixes); driver=ready (send+sleep). Known-failing, consistent with the backchannel-control feasible-Control-declaration verdict.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

- **agent_supports: yes** — Mistral Vibe 2.19.0 renders an interactive tool-permission dialog rendered only in its TUI. When a tool resolves to `ToolPermission.ASK` (no `--yolo`/`--auto-approve`, no stored rule) the `approval_app` Textual widget shows `Permission for the <tool> tool` with the options `Allow once` / `Allow for remainder of this session` / `Always allow` / `Deny` (Esc reject). The held prompt is an in-memory `asyncio.Future` resolved by a widget event and, as the sibling cell `2-19 tool-gate-permission-prompt` established (agent=yes, daemon=incapable via the transcript path), nothing is written to the watched `messages.jsonl` while it is open. That is exactly the transcript-invisible waiting state `backchannel-observe` (read-back, issue #732) exists to surface.

- **daemon_capability: bug** — The read-back subsystem is present and agent-agnostic: `reader.CaptureScreen` (tmux capture-pane / kitty get-text keyed off `session.Launcher`), `backchannel.DetectUI`, `TerminalObserver` polling, and `SessionDetector.handleTerminalUISignal` fusing the edge to `waiting` on the single writer. It fails to surface the signal for vibe today for two FEASIBLE-fix reasons, so this is a product bug, not architectural incapacity:
  1. `TerminalObserver.observe` gates on `o.consent.Granted(adapter, ControlPermissionKey)` (`core/application/services/terminal_observer.go:111`) — the same control consent the write path uses. Vibe's `agent.go` declares **no Control and no control permission** (zero-value `Control` → `SupportsInput=false`; only the `transcripts` observe permission is declared), so that gate can never open and the poller returns before ever capturing the screen.
  2. `DetectUI`'s `trustDialogMarkers` (`core/domain/backchannel/uidetect.go`) are Claude-Code-specific (`"Do you want to proceed?"`, `"Do you trust the files in this folder?"`, `"Do you want to allow this tool"`) and do not include vibe's `"Permission for the … tool"` / `"Allow for remainder of this session"` phrasing — the code comment explicitly invites per-adapter extension "as onboarding cells are recorded."

  The fix is ordinary product work — declare `Control{SupportsInput:true, Interrupt:InterruptCtrlC}` + `agent.ControlPermission` on the vibe adapter (exactly as aider and kiro-cli already do, both assessed yes/full/ready 0.9 for backchannel-control) and add vibe's dialog markers to `DetectUI`. This inherits the backchannel-control sibling verdict (daemon=bug — feasible Control declaration); the read-back path is a strict downstream consumer of the same control-consent chain.

- **driver_capability: ready** — The recipe only needs `send` (a tool-inducing prompt to open the ASK dialog) + `sleep` (let the read-back poll window elapse), both in the vibe scaffold's `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"`. No `keys`/`wait_turn` required (the turn deliberately never completes while the dialog is held, and dismissal is not needed to demonstrate the missing waiting edge).

## Scenario signal

`backchannel-observe` asserts: a tmux/kitty-backed session, backchannel toggle on and "control" granted, is shown **waiting** while a TUI-only trust/permission dialog is on screen (even with no agent hook running), and leaves waiting when it is dismissed. For vibe the load-bearing observable — the `working → waiting` edge derived from read-back — cannot fire because control is never granted (no Control declared) and vibe's dialog is not in the marker set. Hence known_failing: the spec asserts the CORRECT waiting arc; the recording is expected to show the session stay working (the write-gap classification from 2-19), never waiting.

## Why not incapable

Sibling 2-19 is daemon=incapable because the TRANSCRIPT observer fundamentally cannot see the in-memory `asyncio.Future` (nothing in `messages.jsonl`). backchannel-observe uses a DIFFERENT observer — terminal read-back of the rendered screen — which is architecturally able to see the dialog regardless of the transcript. The only blockers are a missing Control declaration and missing markers, both feasible → bug, not incapable.
caveats: PROVISIONAL on emission: the agent-side dialog (agent=yes) is confirmed from vibe source (approval_app.py) and the 2-19 sibling, but the read-back failure mode is derived from reading the gate/marker code — record settles whether the recording shows working (write-gap) or some other non-waiting state.
Recording requires a tmux or kitty backend (read-back is multiplexer/kitty-only); a plain iTerm2/Terminal.app session surfaces no terminal-derived signal even after the fix.

Two marker candidates for the DetectUI fix (from vibe/cli/textual_ui/widgets/approval_app.py): the title prefix 'Permission for the' and the option label 'Allow for remainder of this session' — narrow enough to avoid false positives.
cost is not observable for vibe (parser never reads stats.session_cost; mistral-vibe absent from core/pkg/capacity) — cost_nonzero=false while the model name still displays.

sources.note: vibe declares NO Control (zero-value Control → SupportsInput=false); only the 'transcripts' observe permission — no control permission for the read-back gate to check
observe() returns early unless consent.Granted(adapter, ControlPermissionKey) — read-back is gated on control consent vibe cannot grant
DetectUI trustDialogMarkers are Claude-Code-specific; comment invites per-adapter extension — vibe's 'Permission for the … tool' phrasing not covered
reference: Control{SupportsInput:true, Interrupt:InterruptCtrlC} + ControlPermission → the feasible fix vibe is missing (assessed yes/full/ready 0.9 for backchannel-control)
sibling: vibe DOES render a TUI-only tool-permission dialog (agent=yes); transcript path is incapable — the exact TUI-only state read-back targets
vibe 2.19.0 approval widget: title 'Permission for the <tool> tool'; options Allow once / Allow for remainder of this session / Always allow / Deny; Esc reject
agent-agnostic tmux capture-pane / kitty get-text read-back keyed off session.Launcher — works for vibe once the gate opens

preconditions: mistral-vibe (vibe) v2.19.0 on PATH and authenticated (Mistral API auth persisted on this machine)
the vibe session is hosted in a tmux pane or kitty window (read-back is multiplexer/kitty-only); the Launcher must name a readable backend
vibe launched WITHOUT --yolo/--auto-approve and with no stored approved-rule for the shell tool, so the bash tool resolves to ToolPermission.ASK and opens the approval dialog
irrlichd is recording with the backchannel master-toggle ON

KNOWN-FAILING: vibe declares no control permission, so 'control' cannot be granted — the read-back gate stays shut and the waiting edge will NOT appear until the adapter declares Control and DetectUI learns vibe's markers
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (the driver launches vibe there); the session_<...> transcript dir is minted under ~/.vibe/logs/session/

script.text: Run the shell command: echo hello

verify: the vibe TUI shows the 'Permission for the <tool> tool' approval dialog (Allow once / Allow for remainder of this session / Always allow / Deny) while the send'd prompt's bash tool is held
CORRECT (asserted) behavior: events.jsonl records a state_transition to waiting while the dialog is on screen, derived from terminal read-back, independent of any agent hook
KNOWN FAILURE: because vibe declares no control permission the read-back gate never opens, so no waiting transition occurs — the session stays working (write-gap) for the dialog's duration
observation vector: model=mistral-medium-3.5, tokens_nonzero=true, cost_nonzero=false, agent=mistral-vibe
